sections.label: Section 860
sections.translation: his generous deeds were lavished on a worthless wretch . [ Exeunt HERACLES and Servant .

sections.label: Section 861
sections.translation: Ah me ! I loathe this entering in , and loathe to see my widowed home . Woe , woe is me ! Whither shall I go ? Where stand ? what say ? or what suppress ? Would God that I were dead !

sections.label: Section 865
sections.translation: Surely in an evil hour my mother gave me birth . The dead I envy , and would fain be as they , and long to dwell within their courts . No joy to me to see the light , no joy to tread the earth ;

sections.label: Section 870
sections.translation: such a hostage death hath reft me of and handed o ’ er to Hades .

sections.label: Section 1.872
sections.translation: Move forward , go within the shelter of thy house .

sections.label: Section 1.872b
sections.translation: Woe is me !

sections.label: Section 1.873
sections.translation: Thy sufferings claim these cries of woe .

sections.label: Section 1.873b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.874
sections.translation: Through anguish hast thou gone , full well I know .

sections.label: Section 1.874b
sections.translation: Alas ! alas !

sections.label: Section 1.875
sections.translation: Thou wilt not help the dead one whit .

sections.label: Section 1.875b
sections.translation: O misery !

sections.label: Section 1.876
sections.translation: Nevermore to see thy dear wife face to face is grief indeed .

sections.label: Section 878
sections.translation: Thy words have probed the sore place in my heart . What greater grief can come to man

sections.label: Section 880
sections.translation: than the loss of a faithful wife ? Would I had never married or shared with her my home ! I envy those ’ mongst men who have nor wife nor child . Theirs is but one life ; to grieve for that is no excessive burden ;

sections.label: Section 885
sections.translation: but to see children fall ill and bridal beds emptied by death ’ s ravages is too much to bear , when one might go through life without wife or child .

sections.label: Section 1.889
sections.translation: A fate we cannot cope with is come upon us .

sections.label: Section 1.889b
sections.translation: Woe is me !

sections.label: Section 1.890
sections.original: — πέρας δέ γ ’ οὐδὲν ἀλγέων τίθης .
sections.translation: But thou to sorrow settest no limit .

sections.label: Section 1.890b
sections.translation: Ah ! ah !

sections.label: Section 1.891
sections.translation: ’ Tis hard to bear , but still —

sections.label: Section 1.891b
sections.translation: Woe is me !

sections.label: Section 1.892
sections.translation: Thou art not the first to lose —

sections.label: Section 1.892b
sections.translation: O ! woe is me !

sections.label: Section 1.893
sections.translation: A wife ; misfortune takes a different shape for every man she plagues .

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ὦ μακρὰ πένθη λῦπαί τε φίλων
sections.translation: O the weary sorrow ! O the grief for dear ones dead and gone ! Why didst thou hinder me from plung-ing into the gaping grave , there to lay me down and die with her , my peerless bride ?

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: δύο δ ’ ἀντὶ μιᾶς Ἅιδης ψυχὰς
sections.translation: Then would Hades for that one have gotten these two faithful souls at once , crossing the nether lake together .

sections.label: Section 2.903
sections.translation: I had a kinsman once , within whose home died

sections.label: Section 2.905
sections.translation: his only son , worthy of a father ’ s tears ; yet in spite of that he bore his grief resignedly , childless though he was , his hair already turning grey , himself far on in years , upon

sections.label: Section 2.910
sections.translation: life ’ s downward track .

sections.label: Section 912
sections.translation: O house of mine , how can I enter thee ? how can I live here , now that fortune turns against me ? Ah me ! How wide the gulf ’ twixt then and now !

sections.label: Section 915
sections.translation: Then with torches cut from Pelion ’ s pines , with marriage hymns I entered in , holding my dear wife ’ s hand ; and at our back a crowd of friends with cheerful cries , singing the happy lot of my dead wife and me ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.translation: calling us a noble pair made one , children both of highborn lineage ; but now the voice of woe instead of wedding hymns , and robes of black instead of snowy white usher me

sections.label: Section 925
sections.translation: into my house to my deserted couch .

sections.label: Section 2.926
sections.translation: Hard upon prosperous fortune came this sorrow to thee , a stranger to adversity ; yet hast thou saved thy soul alive .

sections.label: Section 2.930
sections.translation: Thy wife is dead and gone ; her love she leaves with thee . What new thing is here ? Death ere now from many a man hath torn a wife .

sections.label: Section 2.935
sections.translation: My friends , I count my dead wife ’ s lot more blest than mine , for all it seems not so ; for nevermore can sorrow touch her for ever ; all her toil is over , and glorious is her fame . While I , who had no right to live , have passed the bounds of fate

sections.label: Section 2.940
sections.translation: only to live a life of misery ; I know it now . For how shall I endure to enter this my house ? Whom shall I address , by whom be answered back , to find [*] aught joyful in my entering in ? Whither shall I turn ? Within , the desolation will drive me forth ,

sections.label: Section 2.945
sections.original: γυναικὸς εὐνὰς εὖτ ’ ἂν εἰσίδω κενὰς
sections.translation: whensoe ’ er I see my widowed couch , the seat whereon she sat , the floor ail dusty in the house , and my babes falling at my knees with piteous tears for their mother , while my servants mourn the good mistress their house hath lost .

sections.label: Section 2.950
sections.original: τὰ μὲν κατ ’ οἴκους τοιάδʼ · ἔξωθεν δέ με
sections.translation: These are the sorrows in my home , while abroad the marriages among Thessalians and the thronging crowds of women will drive me mad , [*] for I can never bear to gaze upon the compeers of my wife . And whoso is my foe will taunt me thus ,

sections.label: Section 2.955
sections.original: Ἰδοῦ τὸν αἰσχρῶς ζῶνθʼ , ὃς οὐκ ἔτλη θανεῖν ,
sections.translation: Behold him living in his shame , a wretch who quailed at death himself , but of his coward heart gave up his wedded wife instead , and escaped from Hades ; doth he deem himself a man after that ? And he loathes his parents , though himself refused to die . Such ill report shall I to my evils add .

sections.label: Section 2.960
sections.original: ἕξω . τί μοι ζῆν δῆτα κέρδιον , φίλοι ,
sections.translation: What profit , then , my friends , for me to live , in fame and fortune ruined .

sections.label: Section 1.962
sections.translation: Myself have traced the Muses ’ path , have soared amid the stars , have laid my hold on many a theme ,

sections.label: Section 1.965
sections.translation: and yet have found naught stronger than necessity , no spell inscribed on Thracian tablets written there by Orpheus , the sweet singer ,

sections.label: Section 1.970
sections.original: γῆρυς , οὐδ ’ ὅσα Φοῖβος Ἀσκληπιάδαις ἔδωκε
sections.translation: no ! nor aught among the simples culled by Phoebus for the toiling race of men , and given to Asclepius ’ sons .

sections.label: Section 1.973
sections.translation: The only goddess she , whose altar or whose image man cannot approach ;

sections.label: Section 1.975
sections.translation: victims she heedeth not . O come not to me , dread goddess , in greater might than heretofore in my career . Even Zeus requires thy aid to bring to pass whatso he wills .

sections.label: Section 1.980
sections.original: καὶ τὸν ἐν Χαλύβοις δαμάζεις σὺ βίᾳ σίδαρον ,
sections.translation: Thou too it is that by sheer force dost bend the steel among the Chalybes ; nor is there any pity in thy relentless nature .

sections.label: Section 2.984
sections.original: καὶ σ ’ ἐν ἀφύκτοισι χερῶν εἷλε θεὰ δεσμοῖς .
sections.translation: This is the goddess that hath gripped thee too in chains thou canst not ’ scape ;

sections.label: Section 2.985
sections.original: τόλμα δʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἀνάξεις ποτ ’ ἔνερθεν
sections.translation: yet steel thy heart , for all thy weeping ne ’ er will bring to light again the dead from the realms below . Even sons of gods perish in darkness [*]

sections.label: Section 2.990
sections.translation: in the hour of death . We loved her while she was with us , we love her still though dead ; noblest of her sex was she , the wife thou tookest to thy bed .

sections.label: Section 2.995
sections.original: μηδὲ νεκρῶν ὡς φθιμένων χῶμα νομιζέσθω
sections.translation: Her tomb let none regard as the graves of those who die and are no more , but let her have honours equal with the gods , revered by every traveller ;

sections.label: Section 2.1000
sections.translation: and many a one will cross the road and read this verse aloud , This is she that died in days gone by to save her lord ; now is she a spirit blest . Hail , lady revered ; be kind to us !

sections.label: Section 2.1005
sections.translation: Such glad greeting shall she have .

sections.label: Section 1006
sections.original: — καὶ μὴν ὅδʼ , ὡς ἔοικεν , Ἀλκμήνης γόνος ,
sections.translation: But see , Admetus ! yonder , I believe , comes Alcmena ’ s son toward thy hearth .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: φίλον πρὸς ἄνδρα χρὴ λέγειν ἐλευθέρως ,
sections.translation: Admetus , to a friend we should speak freely , not hold our peace and harbour in our hearts complaints .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: σιγῶντʼ . ἐγὼ δὲ σοῖς κακοῖσιν ἠξίουν
sections.translation: I came to thee in thy hour of sorrow and claimed the right to prove myself thy friend , but thou wouldst not tell me that she , thy wife , lay stretched in death ; but didst make me a welcome guest in thy halls , as though thy whole concern was centred on a stranger ’ s loss .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.translation: So I crowned my head and poured drink-offerings to the gods in that thy house of sorrow . Wherefore I do blame thee for this treatment of me , yet would not grieve thee in thy trouble . So now the reason I have turned my steps and come hither again , I will tell .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: λέξω . γυναῖκα τήνδε μοι σῷσον λαβών ,
sections.translation: This lady take and keep for me until I come bringing hither the steeds of Thrace , after I have slain the lord of the Bistones . But should I fare as fare I fain would not , I give her to thee to serve within thy halls .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: πολλῷ δὲ μόχθῳ χεῖρας ἦλθεν εἰς ἐμάς ·
sections.translation: With no small toil she came into my hands . ’ Twas thus : I found folk just appointing an open contest for athletes , well worth a struggle , and there I won her as a prize and brought her thence ; now those who were successful in the lighter contests had
